discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+---
+'mermaid': major
+---
+
+fix: Fixes unsafe or incomplete string escaping in URL manipulation logic by ensuring backslashes are escaped before other characters like parentheses.
discussion_comments.comment_body:
How's this?

```suggestion
fix: Fix incomplete string escaping in URL manipulation logic when `arrowMarkerAbsolute: true` by ensuring all unsafe characters are escaped.
```

- I've removed `unsafe` since this code is already escaped in the HTML. The bug you're fixing means that if you have a URL like: `http://localhost:9000/flowchart.html?hi=world(%20\)`, the arrow markers will now be correct.

  E.g. something like the following would still be a valid SVG, but the `url()` is invalid so the arrows won't be proper (and an additional `url(javascript:)` won't do anything so there shouldn't be any XSS attack possible):

  ```svg
  <path
    d="M196.238,47L196.238,51.167C196.238,55.333,196.238,63.667,196.238,71.333C196.238,79,196.238,86,196.238,89.5L196.238,93"
    id="L_A_B_0"
    class=" edge-thickness-thick edge-pattern-solid edge-thickness-normal edge-pattern-solid flowchart-link"
    style="stroke:gold; stroke-width:4px;fill:none;"
    marker-end="url(http://localhost:9000/flowchart.html?hi=world\(%20\\)#mermaid-1747046847808_flowchart-v2-pointEnd_gold)"></path>
  ```

- I also added ```when `arrowMarkerAbsolute: true` ``` since that's the only case where this bug appears.
- And I changed `ensuring backslashes are escaped` to `ensuring all unsafe characters are escaped` since your PR also fixes escaping `'`/`"`/ and whitespace.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+---

+fix: Fixes unsafe or incomplete string escaping in URL manipulation logic by ensuring backslashes are escaped before other characters like parentheses.
